title: Cognition Space Morphospace PASS: three qualitative clusters realized as three exact QA orbits
_fixture_purpose: Certifies that the qualitative morphospace of Sole, Seoane et al. (2601.12837) — with three identified clusters (basal cognition, neural cognition, human/AI cognition) separated by voids — maps exactly to QA's three orbit families. Voids are algebraically necessary (no intermediate orbit lengths exist in S_9). Agency metric = |reachable set|/|total states| is computable for each orbit.
source_attribution: Ricard Sole, Luisa Seoane et al., 'Cognition spaces and the possible forms of intelligence' (arXiv:2601.12837, Jan 2026). QA formalization: Will Dale + Claude, 2026-04-05.

cluster_orbit_mapping.description: Minimal cognition: chemotaxis, tropisms, single-cell decision-making
Neural cognition: nervous systems, brains, learning
Human and AI cognition: language, abstraction, tool use, artificial general intelligence

void_algebraic_necessity.statement: S_9 admits exactly three orbit families with cycle lengths {1, 8, 24}. No intermediate cycle length exists. The voids between Sole et al.'s clusters are not contingent gaps in a continuous landscape but algebraically necessary consequences of modular Fibonacci dynamics.
void_algebraic_necessity.proof_sketch: T = QA Fibonacci map on (Z/9Z)^2. Orbit lengths must divide the period pi(9)=24. Divisors of 24 that actually appear as orbit lengths in S_9 are {1, 8, 24}. The missing divisors {2,3,4,6,12} do not appear — verified by exhaustive enumeration of all 81 states.

agency_metric.definition: agency(s) = |Orbit(s)| / |S_m| = fraction of total state space reachable by iterating T from state s
agency_metric.note: This gives a constructive, integer-valued agency metric. Sole et al. discuss agency qualitatively; QA makes it exact and enumerable.

enumerability.statement: QA provides the constructive, enumerable cognition space that Sole et al. identify as lacking. Every point in S_m is classifiable by orbit family, cycle length, and agency ratio. The morphospace is finite and completely characterized.

witnesses.note: No orbit of length 12 exists in S_9 despite 12|24. This is a void between satellite (8) and cosmos (24) — the algebraic gap corresponding to Sole et al.'s morphospace void.

cross_references.name: QA Bateson Learning Levels
cross_references.relation: Bateson levels = learning across orbit boundaries; morphospace voids = double bind zones

cross_references.name: QA Keely Triune
cross_references.relation: Three orbit families = Keely's three modes of vibration

cross_references.name: QA Origin of 24
cross_references.relation: Orbit classification via v_3(f) determines the three families
